Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]

The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Buried Individual = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = None | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}}
