Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}} * {{ill|Maruyama revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市) up to rev 99995370
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 大覚寺4号墳 |所属 = [[大覚寺古墳群]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨大覚寺門前堂ノ前町10-1,4 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 1|緯度秒 = 26.17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 40|経度秒 = 54.70 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径28m
高さ4.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]初頭-前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 狐塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|大覚寺古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | label1=1号墳（円山） | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | label2=2号墳（入道塚） | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | label3=3号墳（南天塚） | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | label4=4号墳（狐塚） | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''狐塚古墳'''（きつねづかこふん、大覚寺4号墳）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨大覚寺門前堂ノ前町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[大覚寺古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[大覚寺古墳群]]一覧{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}{{Sfn|花熊祐基|2016}} !順!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!陵墓・史跡 |- |1||[[円山古墳 (京都市)|1号墳
（円山古墳）]]||[[円墳]]||直径50m||6c後半||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |2||3号墳
（南天塚古墳）||円墳|| ||6c後半-末||（埋没） |- |rowspan=2|3||'''4号墳
（狐塚古墳）'''||円墳||直径28m||rowspan=2|7c初頭-前半||なし |- |2号墳
（入道塚古墳）||[[方墳]]||25×30m|||陵墓参考地 |}
[[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}]] [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部の[[有栖川]][[扇状地]]に築造された古墳である。一帯では4基の古墳が分布して大覚寺古墳群を形成し、本古墳はそのうち最南端に位置する。古くから石室が開口し、墳丘周囲裾部は削平されて宅地化が進んでいる。[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に測量調査が、[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）以降に測量・発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で（ただし方形の可能性も残る{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}）、直径28メートル・高さ4.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては直径45メートル以上におよぶと推定される{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。チャート自然石の巨石を用いて構築された石室になる。副葬品は明らかでないが、墳丘南側（石室前面裾）の発掘調査では[[須恵器]]片（坏身・坏蓋・高坏：TK43-209型式期）が検出されている{{Sfn|第5次調査報告|2020}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭-前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|花熊祐基|2016}}。 現在では石室内への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 少なくとも[[近世]]期には開口（土師器皿の出土）{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）まで、石室開口部前面に稲荷社の祠が所在{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量・石室実測調査：第1次調査（[[京都大学]]考古学研究室、[[1971年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）、石室実測および羨道・前庭部の発掘調査：第2次調査（[[龍谷大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[2016年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[2015年]]度（平成27年度）、墳丘測量調査：第3次調査。方墳の可能性を示唆（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2017年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第3次調査報告|2017}}。 * 2016年度（平成28年度）、墳丘東側の発掘調査：第4次調査（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第4次調査報告|2018}}。 * 2018年度（平成30年度）、墳丘南側の発掘調査：第5次調査。[[須恵器]]片の出土（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2020年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第5次調査報告|2020}}。 * [[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）、墳丘周辺試掘調査（京都市文化財保護課、[[2022年]]に報告）{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * 石室全長：現存6.8メートル * 玄室：長さ3.8メートル、幅2.0-2.4メートル、現在高さ2.1-2.3メートル * 羨道：現存長さ3.0メートル、幅1.5メートル、現在高さ1.2メートル 石室の石材は[[チャート]]で（一部の小型石材に[[砂岩]]等）、自然石を使用して構築される。玄室の奥壁は各段1石の3段積みであるが、1段目は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとせず、3段目はやや持ち送る。側壁は各段2石の3段積みと推定されるが、奥壁同様に1段目は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとしない。縦目地・横目地を意識した様子はあまり見受けられず、隙間には小型石材を充填する。前壁は持ち送らず垂直に積む。玄門部の立柱石は1石。羨道は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとしない{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2022|chapter=大覚寺古墳群|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告 令和3年度]|publisher=京都市文化市民局|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 龍谷大学発行 ** {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=大覚寺4号墳第2次発掘・測量調査報告|date=2016|journal=龍谷大学考古学実習|publisher=龍谷大学文学部考古学実習室|volume=|number=12|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査報告|2016}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=花熊祐基「考察」|ref={{Harvid|花熊祐基|2016}}}}。 ** {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=大覚寺4号墳第3次測量調査報告|date=2017|journal=龍谷大学考古学実習|publisher=龍谷大学文学部考古学実習室|volume=|number=13|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査報告|2017}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=大覚寺4号墳（狐塚古墳）第4次発掘調査報告|title=考古学実習・文化財実習報告書 第1集|publisher=龍谷大学文学部歴史学科文化遺産学専攻|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査報告|2018}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2020|chapter=大覚寺4号墳（狐塚古墳）第5次発掘調査報告|title=考古学実習・文化財実習報告書 第3集|publisher=龍谷大学文学部歴史学科文化遺産学専攻|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|第5次調査報告|2020}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 狐塚古墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大覚寺古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:きつねつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:太秦]]

（入道塚古墳）||[[方墳]]||25×30m|||陵墓参考地 |}
[[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}]] [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部の[[有栖川]][[扇状地]]に築造された古墳である。一帯では4基の古墳が分布して大覚寺古墳群を形成し、本古墳はそのうち最南端に位置する。古くから石室が開口し、墳丘周囲裾部は削平されて宅地化が進んでいる。[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に測量調査が、[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）以降に測量・発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で（ただし方形の可能性も残る{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}）、直径28メートル・高さ4.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては直径45メートル以上におよぶと推定される{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。チャート自然石の巨石を用いて構築された石室になる。副葬品は明らかでないが、墳丘南側（石室前面裾）の発掘調査では[[須恵器]]片（坏身・坏蓋・高坏：TK43-209型式期）が検出されている{{Sfn|第5次調査報告|2020}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭-前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|花熊祐基|2016}}。 現在では石室内への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 少なくとも[[近世]]期には開口（土師器皿の出土）{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）まで、石室開口部前面に稲荷社の祠が所在{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量・石室実測調査：第1次調査（[[京都大学]]考古学研究室、[[1971年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）、石室実測および羨道・前庭部の発掘調査：第2次調査（[[龍谷大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[2016年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * [[2015年]]度（平成27年度）、墳丘測量調査：第3次調査。方墳の可能性を示唆（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2017年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第3次調査報告|2017}}。 * 2016年度（平成28年度）、墳丘東側の発掘調査：第4次調査（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第4次調査報告|2018}}。 * 2018年度（平成30年度）、墳丘南側の発掘調査：第5次調査。[[須恵器]]片の出土（龍谷大学文学部考古学研究室、[[2020年]]に報告）{{Sfn|第5次調査報告|2020}}。 * [[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）、墳丘周辺試掘調査（京都市文化財保護課、[[2022年]]に報告）{{Sfn|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 * 石室全長：現存6.8メートル * 玄室：長さ3.8メートル、幅2.0-2.4メートル、現在高さ2.1-2.3メートル * 羨道：現存長さ3.0メートル、幅1.5メートル、現在高さ1.2メートル 石室の石材は[[チャート]]で（一部の小型石材に[[砂岩]]等）、自然石を使用して構築される。玄室の奥壁は各段1石の3段積みであるが、1段目は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとせず、3段目はやや持ち送る。側壁は各段2石の3段積みと推定されるが、奥壁同様に1段目は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとしない。縦目地・横目地を意識した様子はあまり見受けられず、隙間には小型石材を充填する。前壁は持ち送らず垂直に積む。玄門部の立柱石は1石。羨道は土砂の堆積のため詳らかとしない{{Sfn|第2次調査報告|2016}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2022|chapter=大覚寺古墳群|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告 令和3年度]|publisher=京都市文化市民局|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|京都市内遺跡試掘調査報告|2022}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 龍谷大学発行 ** {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=大覚寺4号墳第2次発掘・測量調査報告|date=2016|journal=龍谷大学考古学実習|publisher=龍谷大学文学部考古学実習室|volume=|number=12|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査報告|2016}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=花熊祐基「考察」|ref={{Harvid|花熊祐基|2016}}}}。 ** {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=大覚寺4号墳第3次測量調査報告|date=2017|journal=龍谷大学考古学実習|publisher=龍谷大学文学部考古学実習室|volume=|number=13|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査報告|2017}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=大覚寺4号墳（狐塚古墳）第4次発掘調査報告|title=考古学実習・文化財実習報告書 第1集|publisher=龍谷大学文学部歴史学科文化遺産学専攻|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査報告|2018}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2020|chapter=大覚寺4号墳（狐塚古墳）第5次発掘調査報告|title=考古学実習・文化財実習報告書 第3集|publisher=龍谷大学文学部歴史学科文化遺産学専攻|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|第5次調査報告|2020}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 狐塚古墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大覚寺古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:きつねつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:太秦]]

Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群}} * {{ill|Maruyama Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|12=draft}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=draft}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|12=draft}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世|12=draft}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=draft}} * Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|qq=draft}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|qq=draft}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|qq=draft}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世|qq=draft}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kofun|ja|円山古墳|qq=draft}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 Kyoto City Archaeological Report: Reiwa 3]|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural and Citizen Affairs Bureau|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}}} * Published by Ryukoku University ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=2nd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Second Survey Report|2016}}}} ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=3rd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2017|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Third Survey Report|2017}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2018|chapter=4th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 1|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2020|chapter=5th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 3|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}}} {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun Kyoto}} * {{citation needed|Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐塚古墳 Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft}}, Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|qq=draft}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}, around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Edo period|ja|近世|qq=draft}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kofun|ja|円山古墳|qq=draft}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 Kyoto City Archaeological Report: Reiwa 3]|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural and Citizen Affairs Bureau|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}}} * Published by Ryukoku University ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=2nd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Second Survey Report|2016}}}} ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=3rd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2017|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Third Survey Report|2017}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2018|chapter=4th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 1|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2020|chapter=5th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 3|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|version=99995370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Taizan and Sagano Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo-ku]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)]] [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|qq=}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the [[:en:Early modern period|Edo period]] (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kofun|ja|円山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 Kyoto City Archaeological Report: Reiwa 3]|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural and Citizen Affairs Bureau|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}}} * Published by Ryukoku University ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=2nd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Second Survey Report|2016}}}} ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=3rd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2017|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Third Survey Report|2017}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2018|chapter=4th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 1|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2020|chapter=5th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 3|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|version=99995370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|qq=}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the [[:en:Early modern period|Edo period]] (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kofun|ja|円山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 Kyoto City Archaeological Report: Reiwa 3]|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural and Citizen Affairs Bureau|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}}} * Published by Ryukoku University ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=2nd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Second Survey Report|2016}}}} ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=3rd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2017|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Third Survey Report|2017}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2018|chapter=4th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 1|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2020|chapter=5th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 3|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|version=99995370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho 10-1, 4 | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = [[Sue pottery|ja|須恵器]] | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|qq=}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since [[1970]] (Showa 45) and intensified after [[2014]] (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the early to mid-[[7th century]]{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the [[:en:Early modern period|Edo period]] (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By [[1936]] (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in [[1971]] by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in [[2016]] by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in [[2017]] by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in [[2018]]){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in [[2020]]), yielding [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in [[2022]] by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kofun|ja|円山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122004 Kyoto City Archaeological Report: Reiwa 3]|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural and Citizen Affairs Bureau|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}}} * Published by Ryukoku University ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=2nd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Second Survey Report|2016}}}} ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author=|title=3rd Survey Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|date=2017|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harvid|Third Survey Report|2017}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2018|chapter=4th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 1|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2020|chapter=5th Excavation Report of Daikakuji 4th Kofun|title=Archaeological Practices and Heritage Reports Vol. 3|publisher=Ryukoku University|ref={{Harvid|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|version=99995370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|
