[[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom | part_of = {{ill|Q115921975|WD=Q115921975|1=Daikakuji Kofun Group}} }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|1=Arisugawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997746}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Showa 45) and intensified after {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the early to mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Edo period|1=Early modern period}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in {{ill|2016|en|2016|WD=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}}), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in {{ill|2022|en|2022|WD=Q49625|lt=2022|1=2022}} by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:狐塚古墳 (京都市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom | part_of = {{ill|Q115921975|WD=Q115921975|1=Daikakuji Kofun Group}} }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|1=Arisugawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997746}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Showa 45) and intensified after {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the early to mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Edo period|1=Early modern period}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in {{ill|2016|en|2016|WD=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}}), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in {{ill|2022|en|2022|WD=Q49625|lt=2022|1=2022}} by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kitsunezuka Kofun | image = File:Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg | Other names = Daikakuji 4th Kofun | Part of = [[Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群]] | location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 1| coordinates = 26.17 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 40| coordinates = 54.70 | omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | area = Diameter: 28m
Height: 4.5m | burial = Double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] | burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | Kofuns = | built = Early to mid {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Kitsunezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom | part_of = {{ill|Q115921975|qid=Q115921975|1=Daikakuji Kofun Group}} }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|1|29.8|N|135|40|50.0|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Daikakuji Kofun Group Distribution map}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | map_dot_label1=1st Kofun (Maruyama) | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|1|31.63|N|135|40|53.04|E}} | map_dot_label2=2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka) | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|1|29.00|N|135|40|49.02|E}} | map_dot_label3=3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka) | map_dot_label-pos3=top | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|1|26.17|N|135|40|54.70|E}} | map_dot_label4=4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka) | map_dot_label-pos4=top | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 }} '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (狐塚古墳, Daikakuji 4th Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Saga Daikakuji Monzen Donomaecho, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. The tumulus, part of the {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}, is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == This tumulus, located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}} within the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|1=Arisugawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23997746}} alluvial fan, is the southernmost kofun of the Daikakuji Kofun Group. Over time, the stone chamber has been exposed, and the area around the mound's base has been leveled for residential use. Surveys and excavations have been conducted since {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Showa 45) and intensified after {{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26). The mound is circular in type (though there is a possibility of it being rectangular{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}), measuring 28 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. A surrounding moat extends the total diameter of the kofun to over 45 meters{{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, constructed from large natural stones of chert. Excavation at the southern side of the mound revealed fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (TK43-209 type vessels){{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the early to mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016}}. Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Excavation History == * Opened since at least the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Edo period|1=Early modern period}} (earthenware dishes were discovered){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * By {{ill|1936|en|1936|qid=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Showa 11), a small Inari shrine was located in front of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Measurement and detailed survey of the mound and stone chamber (1st survey, reported in {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} by Kyoto University Archaeology Lab). * '''2014 (Heisei 26):''' Measurement and excavation of the antechamber and forecourt (2nd survey, reported in {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} by Ryukoku University){{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Measurement survey of the mound (3rd survey, reported in {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} by Ryukoku University), suggesting a rectangular type{{Sfn|Third Survey Report|2017}}. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Excavation of the eastern side of the mound (4th survey, reported in {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}){{Sfn|Fourth Survey Report|2018}}. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Excavation of the southern side of the mound (5th survey, reported in {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}}), yielding {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments{{Sfn|Fifth Survey Report|2020}}. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Test excavation around the mound (reported in {{ill|2022|en|2022|qid=Q49625|lt=2022|1=2022}} by Kyoto City designation1 Protection Division){{Sfn|Kyoto City Archaeological Report|2022}}. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}: * Total Length: 6.8 meters (extant) * Main Chamber: Length 3.8 meters, Width 2.0-2.4 meters, Current Height 2.1-2.3 meters * Passageway: Extant Length 3.0 meters, Width 1.5 meters, Current Height 1.2 meters The chamber is constructed using natural chert stones (with smaller sandstone stones in parts). The rear wall of the main chamber is three tiers of single stones. The side walls are believed to be three tiers of double stones, though sediment obscures the first tier. Vertical and horizontal joints are inconsistently aligned, with small stones used to fill gaps. The front wall is vertically aligned without overlap. The entrance has a single upright pillar stone{{Sfn|Second Survey Report|2016}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}} *
